behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model diarization quality/content, not just local wiring. A stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide real failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets a single quality boundary ('speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels'), not a required multi-agentic sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT provider with speaker diarization enabled; assert transcript includes stable speaker labels and that downstream attribution input preserves those labels per utterance.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text (single local logic boundary). No required OS/network/subprocess/real model dependency in the behavior itself. Rejected Integration: no concrete cross-module handoff failure is stated beyond parser correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments; assert output objects contain normalized assignee and task text fields exactly as schema expects, including edge punctuation/format cases.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM content selection/completeness (LLM-DEP). Deterministic mocks can always emit compliant action items and miss omissions seen in real generations. Rejected Workflow: behavior text does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence; it can be validated against a fixed transcript + one live summarization step.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts with known assignee-task ground truth; call real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text scopes to routing logic after decisions are already identified ('routes identified decisions'), which is local deterministic mapping. Rejected Agentic: no requirement here to validate model judgment quality for identifying decisions; only schema routing.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing function with pre-identified decision records and narrative fields; assert all decision records land in `decisions` schema nodes and none leak into narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is model output quality/placement: whether real LLM surfaces decision content in the correct section. Stubbed outputs hide this failure. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be tested from transcript-to-summary in one model decision; it does not require validating a multi-agentic chain explicitly.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with clear decision statements; run real summarization call and assert those decision statements are represented in `decisions` entries and absent as narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (per-segment transcription/summarization plus merge), with ordering/state handoff across stages (MULTI-STEP). Rejected Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires chained sequential processing and merged final coherence, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording; run end-to-end segmented mode with real model calls, assert segment boundaries/order are respected, all segment outputs are merged, and final structured summary is coherent and complete across segments.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly end-to-end across multiple stages with real recording and model-driven steps; this is a full agent pipeline check. Any lower level would miss cross-stage failures and model-stage interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like E2E test: upload real meeting audio through public entrypoint, execute real transcription and summarization, and assert successful structured delivery with no human retries/intervention.
